title: OUT_FULL_16 and OUT_NO_ORIENT share the same bitmask value (8), silently suppressing orientation when full-16 output is requested
description: Both `OUT_FULL_16` (line 551) and `OUT_NO_ORIENT` (line 556) are defined as `8u32`. Any caller that passes `output_flags | OUT_FULL_16` (e.g. 7|8 = 15 for 'M3 full-16') will also have the `OUT_NO_ORIENT` bit set, causing `process_orf_impl` and `process_dng_impl` to skip `apply_orientation` on the full RGB8 output. The callers and docstring for `process_orf_with_flags` explicitly advise passing 15 for M3 full-16, which means all M3 full-16 outputs are silently unrotated. This is a logic error introduced by reusing a bit position for two unrelated features.

title: now_ms() re-executes the full JS-global lookup on every call from rayon worker threads when built with parallel-wasm
description: The `now_ms` function caches a `web_sys::Performance` object in a `thread_local! { OnceCell<Performance> }`. On rayon worker threads (spawned by wasm-bindgen-rayon), `web_sys::window()` returns `None` and the `WorkerGlobalScope::dyn_into` also fails, so `perf` is always `None`. Because `cell.set(perf)` is only called when `perf` is `Some`, the `OnceCell` for each rayon thread stays empty forever. Every timing call from a rayon context re-runs the failed DOM lookups, adding unnecessary JS-FFI overhead per timing sample across all parallel tone/demosaic work that uses `now_ms`. The correct fix is to set the cell to a sentinel or call `Date::now()` directly from rayon contexts without attempting the DOM lookup.

title: Unsafe raw pointer advance in v_pass_tiled lacks a bounds check for the right-edge partial tile, risking out-of-bounds reads on malformed synthetic data
description: The `v_pass_tiled` function computes `sp = src.as_ptr().add(row + x0 * 3)` and then advances it `tile * 3` times via unchecked `sp.add(1)`. The invariant relies on `x1 = (x0 + TILE).min(w)` and `tile = x1 - x0`, so the maximum pointer position reached is `row + (x0 + tile) * 3 - 1`. For the last tile at the right edge, `x0 + tile == w`, so the last read is `src[row + w*3 - 1]`, which is within bounds as long as `src.len() >= (yi * w + w) * 3`. This holds for valid calls but is not checked inside the unsafe block. The safety comment says 'SAFETY: tile, x0, yi validated by caller contract + clamp; bench synthetic data,' but there is no explicit assertion. If ever called with a `src` slice shorter than `h * w * 3` this is UB. Bench-only context mitigates severity.

title: bench_perf_flipflop mutates rgb16 in-place on flip rounds, meaning flop rounds measure a different (already-processed) input than the original synthetic data
description: The flip branch (serial while-loop, lines 486-493) modifies `rgb16` in place by blending it with `blurred`. After round 0 (flip), `rgb16` no longer contains the original synthetic values; rounds 2, 4, 6 (the remaining flips) operate on already-blended data, and rounds 1, 3, 5, 7 (flops) operate on partially-blended data from prior flips. The benchmark is intended to compare the same operation at serial vs parallel speed, but the input diverges across rounds, making median timings slightly less representative. It also means the flip-flop result is not idempotent across multiple benchmark runs in the same process (e.g. if `bench_perf_flipflop` were called twice). Low severity because it is a benchmark-only binary and the timing difference introduced is small.

title: Long-running WASM pipeline functions (decode_orf_raw, decode_dng_raw) have no cancellation path for the parallel-wasm build
description: When built with `parallel-wasm` and rayon, the demosaic and tone stages can spawn many rayon threads. There is no mechanism to cancel an in-progress decode if the JS caller navigates away or calls another decode. The only isolation is that each wasm-bindgen export is synchronous and JS is single-threaded, but rayon worker threads may still be running decompress or demosaic work from a prior call when the next export is invoked. Adding a cooperative cancellation flag (e.g. an `AtomicBool` checked between rayon chunks) would prevent wasted CPU when the caller has already moved on.
